content.claim: Within-school achievement variance was not significantly related to tracking age (Suchań and Bergmüller 2010), suggesting tracking achieves between-school rather than within-school homogenization
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Single study; finding contradicts implicit efficiency rationale for tracking but has not been widely replicated
Does not measure teacher-practice differences or curriculum alignment within schools despite heterogeneity
Limited to specific datasets and performance domains tested
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
